reasoning: VIX at 18.66 (MID regime) falling -8.03% signals rapid fear recession and premium compression, a strong bullish tailwind for call buyers. Overnight gap is flat (-0.18), so no overnight bullish reinforcement, but sector rotation is explicitly risk-on (XLK +0.61%, XLF +0.73% leading; XLE -1.48% lagging). Zero macro events today removes all event-risk noise. FOMC meeting not until 2026-03-18. Environment is clean for directional entry. Confidence moderated slightly due to flat overnight gap (would expect gap-up to reinforce bullish bias more strongly) and MIXED ribbon stack (not yet strongly aligned). Overall backdrop favors bullish 0DTE setups through EOD.
key_observations: VIX fell 8.03% overnight (est. 20.3 yesterday to 18.66 today) — fear recession indicates premium is compressing and volatility sellers are exiting.
Sector breadth: XLK + XLF leading (tech + financials), XLE lagging (energy). Classic risk-on rotation into growth/financials.
Zero macro events on calendar for 2026-02-18; next high-impact event is FOMC on 2026-03-18 (28 days out, not a near-term concern).

Overnight gap is flat — no gap-up bullish thrust from Asia/futures, but also no gap-down fear. Price starting session unchanged from close signals neutral overnight but within a bullish-drift day (pre-market ribbon MIXED, not yet aligned).
